description: Dependency Management - Support external .jar libraries in a libs/ folder within the code project. Scan this folder during build and add JARs to compilation classpath and D8 dexing.
steps: Add `getLibsPath()` method to app/src/main/java/pro/sketchware/codeproject/model/CodeProject.java: Return `getProjectMyscPath() + File.separator + "app" + File.separator + "libs"`. This follows the same structure as the source path (app/src/main/...).

Modify app/src/main/java/pro/sketchware/codeproject/template/CodeProjectTemplate.java in the `generate()` method: Add `FileUtil.makeDir(project.getLibsPath())` to create the libs/ directory as part of the project scaffold.

Modify app/src/main/java/pro/sketchware/codeproject/build/CodeProjectBuilder.java - add a helper method `private List<File> getLibraryJars()` that scans `new File(project.getLibsPath())` for all .jar files (not recursive, just top-level). Return an empty list if the directory does not exist.
In `compileJava()`: After building the classpath StringBuilder, iterate over `getLibraryJars()` and append each jar path to the classpath with ':' separator.
In `compileKotlin()`: After building the classpath StringBuilder, iterate over `getLibraryJars()` and append each jar path to the classpath with ':' separator.

In `createDex()`: After collecting programFiles from classesDir, also add all .jar files from `getLibraryJars()` to the programFiles collection (D8 can dex jar files directly). Use `file.toPath()` for each jar.
acceptance_criteria: CodeProject.getLibsPath() returns the correct path to the libs/ directory
CodeProjectTemplate.generate() creates a libs/ directory in the project scaffold
compileJava() includes all .jar files from libs/ in the classpath
compileKotlin() includes all .jar files from libs/ in the classpath
createDex() includes all .jar files from libs/ as program files for D8
verification: Run ./gradlew assembleDebug in the project root and confirm it completes without compilation errors
findings: The build/ pattern in .gitignore matches the 'build' path segment in codeproject/build/. Need to use git add -f for files in that directory since they are already tracked.
